title: phyllota aspera
intro: Phyllota aspera, also known as Rough-leaved Phyllota, is a shrub native to Australia, characterized by its hairy, rough leaves and small, blue or purple flowers. Traditionally used in folk medicine, Phyllota aspera is believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions. The plant is said to have numerous health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as a diuretic to treat urinary problems. Phyllota aspera is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal purposes, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
